entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The second most important grain legume worldwide, cultivated mainly on marginal soils.

entities.name: MARGINAL SOILS
entities.description: Soils characterized by low productivity where chickpea is mainly cultivated.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A condition where phosphorus levels are insufficient, restricting chickpea yields.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS-ACQUISITION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to acquire phosphorus from the soil.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant utilizes phosphorus for growth and development.

entities.name: P-FERTILIZER APPLICATION
entities.description: The process of applying phosphorus fertilizer to crops to improve yield.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION MAPPING
entities.description: An approach used to determine genetic loci and genes associated with specific traits.

entities.name: ROOT ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The spatial configuration and morphology of the root system.

entities.name: ROOT TRAITS
entities.description: Morphological characteristics of roots associated with phosphorus acquisition and use efficiency.

entities.name: GENERALIZED LINEAR MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model used to identify marker-trait associations.

entities.name: MIXED LINEAR MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model used to identify marker-trait associations.

entities.name: FARMCPU
entities.description: Fixed and random model circulating probability unification, a statistical model used to identify marker-trait associations.

entities.name: MARKER-TRAIT ASSOCIATIONS
entities.description: The statistical association between a genetic marker and a phenotypic trait.

entities.name: CA1_12310101
entities.description: A single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) locus located on chromosome Ca1.

entities.name: CA1
entities.description: The specific chromosome where the SNP locus Ca1_12310101 is located.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL P-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A specific measure of the plant's efficiency in using phosphorus.

entities.name: SHOOT DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the shoot after all water has been removed.

entities.name: SHOOT P CONTENT
entities.description: The total amount of phosphorus present in the shoot.

entities.name: SHOOT P CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The concentration of phosphorus within the shoot tissue.

entities.name: NAD KINASE 2
entities.description: A gene identified as being related to shoot phosphorus concentration.

entities.name: DYNAMIN-RELATED PROTEIN 1C
entities.description: A gene identified as being related to shoot phosphorus concentration.

entities.name: FASCICLINLIKE ARABINOGALACTAN PROTEIN
entities.description: A gene identified as being related to physiological phosphorus-use efficiency.

entities.name: SPECIFIC ROOT LENGTH
entities.description: A root morphological trait representing root length per unit mass.

entities.name: 4-COUMARATE–COA LIGASE 1
entities.description: A gene identified as being related to specific root length.

entities.name: MANGANESE CONCENTRATION IN MATURE LEAVES
entities.description: The concentration of manganese found in the mature leaves of the plant.

entities.name: ABC1 FAMILY PROTEIN
entities.description: A gene identified as being related to manganese concentration in mature leaves.
